headline: OpenAI CEO acknowledges rising token costs becoming significant problem
body: Sam Altman publicly admitted that AI token costs are escalating into a material issue for OpenAI, signaling that per-inference expenses are straining the company's unit economics. For healthcare organizations deploying large language models in clinical workflows, this cost acknowledgment matters because it affects vendor pricing strategy and the ROI calculations for AI-assisted documentation, diagnostic support, and EHR integration projects. Healthcare systems evaluating OpenAI's API for production use should now factor in potential price adjustments and consider negotiating longer-term contracts before margins compress further. The visibility around token cost pressure also creates openings for competing inference providers to position lower-cost alternatives for healthcare's cost-sensitive value-based care environment.
